channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Layoffs begin at US health agencies responsible for research, tracking disease and regulating food
item.description: Employees across the massive U.S. Health and Human Services Department began receiving notices of dismissal on Tuesday in an overhaul ultimately expected to lay off up to 10,000 people.
item.dc:creator: CARLA K. JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Trump administration sued over decision to rescind billions in health funding
item.description: A coalition of state attorneys general sued the Trump administration Tuesday over its decision to cut $11 billion in federal funds that go toward COVID-19 initiatives and various public health projects across the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New Jersey Sen. Booker presses his marathon speech against Trump's agenda past 16 hours and counting
item.description: New Jersey Democratic Sen. Cory Booker has pressed his marathon speech against President Donald Trump’s agenda past 16 hours and counting.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Top Dems sue Trump over executive order requiring proof of citizenship in federal elections
item.description: President Donald Trump's executive order to overhaul the nation's elections now faces two legal challenges in the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why does the US restrict its presidents to 2 terms? A look at the tradition Trump is questioning
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Only one person in U.S. history has defied the two-term example set by the first president, George Washington. And voters responded by forbidding future presidents from being elected more than twice.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin voters to choose top education official and consider voter ID amendment
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Wisconsin’s top education official, who will guide policies affecting K-12 schools during President Donald Trump’s second term, will be elected Tuesday in a race between the teachers union-backed incumbent and a Republican-supported critic.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Legislation aimed at protecting Florida state parks stuck in committee
item.description: What all started with a leaked proposal to build golf courses and pickleball courts at state parks last year has now resulted in legislation that aims to protect natural grounds from those very acts.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration says it deported 17 more ‘violent criminals’ to El Salvador
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration said Monday that it has deported 17 more “violent criminals” from the Tren de Aragua and MS-13 gangs to El Salvador, as it doubles down on a policy of removing people from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and MARCOS ALEMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin Supreme Court candidates make final push amid high spending, voting
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — President Donald Trump's preferred candidate for Wisconsin Supreme Court and his Democratic-backed challenger made a final blitz across the state on Monday, the day before voting concludes in a race where early turnout has surged and spending is nearing $100 million.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hegseth orders fitness standards to be gender neutral for combat jobs. Many already are
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has ordered the military to make fitness standards for all combat jobs gender neutral, formalizing a process that largely exists for many of those jobs already.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin Supreme Court race draws national attention, record out-of-state money
item.description: Zac Schultz, a reporter with PBS Wisconsin’s “Here & Now” program, has been closely covering the race and joined MPR News host Cathy Wurzer on Morning Edition with the highlights.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Suspected Tren de Aragua, MS-13 members sent to El Salvador
item.description: Suspected Venezuelan gang members and criminals were among the 17 migrants sent to El Salvador by the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: How Biden's frailties hampered Harris
item.description: Democratic Party officials and White House staffers were well aware of former President Biden’s frailties ahead of his choice to end his ill-fated reelection bid last summer, according to exclusive excerpts of a new book released to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota loses $27.5 million in behavioral health funds as part of Trump administration cuts
item.description: Overnight this week, the Minnesota Department of Human Services lost hundreds of contracts, amounting to $27.5 million, after massive federal cuts from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crann and Gretchen Brown, MPR News

item.title: Trump tariff tumult has ripples for sporting goods, puts costly hockey gear in price-hike crosshair
item.description: The sports equipment industry is no exception to the headaches being caused throughout the U.S. commercial ecosystem by the promise of 25 percent tariffs on Canadian imports by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to allow deportations under Alien Enemies Act
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union and Democracy Forward sued the Trump administration over its use of the wartime law to quickly deport people, which they say violates due process.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán, MPR News

item.title: Appeals court won't lift Boasberg's order blocking Alien Enemies Act
item.description: A federal appeals court in a 2-1 decision Wednesday refused to lift U.S. District Judge James Boasberg’s order blocking the Trump administration from swiftly deporting migrants under the Alien Enemies Act. The Justice Department had urged the three-judge panel on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Florida 'inmate bill of rights' would require improving prison conditions
item.description: A Tampa Bay lawmaker is fighting for legislation that would protect the rights of prisoners in the state's corrections system.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
